items.problem: The primary CI push trigger names bassam and master but not lian, so lian head commits have no authoritative same-commit CI execution.
items.rootCause: Branch-specific workflow configuration was not reconciled when execution moved to lian.
acceptance: CI triggers on push to lian
Required jobs retain fail-closed aggregation
A run is produced for the same immutable lian commit

items.problem: DSH service.manifest declares PARTIALLY_BOUND/FIX_REQUIRED and global screen/runtime readiness false while surface-map and runtime-map declare broad runtime verification.
items.rootCause: Readiness is duplicated across registries with different evidence semantics and no enforced reconciliation invariant.
acceptance: One explicit readiness model and precedence rule
No contradictory capability or surface claim
Every runtime claim references same-commit executable evidence
Guard fails on future contradiction

items.problem: Several required-looking scripts catch failures and return success, allowing false green results; a performance smoke targets host localhost:8080 instead of the governed DSH host port.
items.rootCause: Diagnostic wrappers and enforceable gates are not consistently separated, and host/container port contexts are mixed.
acceptance: Required test/guard/performance scripts propagate non-zero exits
Optional diagnostics are explicitly named diagnostics
Host DSH smoke defaults to http://localhost:58080

items.problem: The committed enforcement snapshot targets bassam and does not prove lian protection, required checks, same-commit CI, or independent reviewers.
items.rootCause: A historical branch-scoped snapshot is present without live lian verification.
acceptance: Snapshot targets lian and is refreshed only from live GitHub facts
Unproven fields remain NOT_PROVEN
No high-risk closure without independent identities and required checks

items.problem: DSH declares multiple active OpenAPI fragments, while central generation and CI drift comparison visibly cover only the main DSH contract plus separately handled catalog output.
items.rootCause: Contract activation, generated-client registration, and CI drift coverage evolved independently.
acceptance: Every active contract has an owned generated output or explicit composition rule
Every output is drift-checked
Routes and consumers are binding-checked
No unstable or malformed operation identifiers remain

items.problem: WLT implementation reports active local/staging mutation support but journey runtime and production mutations are not verified.
items.rootCause: Code readiness, simulator evidence, provider-lab evidence, and production financial authorization are separate scopes.
acceptance: DSH holds references only
Local/staging journeys pass with idempotency and reconciliation
Production remains fail-closed without real provider configuration and independent financial/release evidence

items.problem: Broad screen and surface readiness claims are not yet reconciled with navigation reachability, actor permissions, backend mutation, persistence, readback, and cross-surface refresh evidence.
items.rootCause: Static binding and historical evidence paths were promoted to runtime-level language.
acceptance: Every required UI control has an end-to-end binding
Every excluded surface has a Product Truth reason
Negative and permission paths pass
No local commercial or financial truth
